destinations.evidence: The node add-ons create a concrete shared SpcVerifier and pass it to the Ritual async RPC module.
The RPC context owns the shared verifier, syncs its registry from state, and requires verify_single success before accepting an async result submission.

equivalence_argument: RPC async result submission is backed by the same concrete SpcVerifier type created during add-on launch; verification failure returns a failed response before pool submission.
rationale: The subject says the RPC endpoint now uses the ritual-spc-verifier crate, and the diff removes reqwest URL construction while adding SpcVerifier::new_from_env, spc_verifier storage, and verify_single calls. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: RitualApi constructs a SpcVerifier from environment-derived configuration and an ExecutorRegistry, and verify_result validates async submissions by building a SpcVerificationRequest and calling SpcVerifier::verify_single rather than POSTing directly to a verification URL.
suggested_falsification_tests: Instantiate RitualApi with a mocked SpcVerifier path, submit an async result, and assert a SpcVerificationRequest with previous block hash/timestamp is passed to verify_single; assert no direct HTTP POST to RITUAL_SIDECAR_BASE_URL is performed by the RPC layer.
summary: RPC async result submission uses a shared SpcVerifier before accepting results.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Instantiate RitualApi with a mocked SpcVerifier path, submit an async result, and assert a SpcVerificationRequest with previous block hash/timestamp is passed to verify_single; assert no direct HTTP POST to RITUAL_SIDECAR_BASE_URL is performed by the RPC layer.
title: RPC async verification uses SpcVerifier
